qas.q: In addition to EOD net, what other nets should be programmed into our radios?
qas.a: Airfield Control Tower, Fire/Crash, CE, security Forces and supported ranges

qas.q: Who does EOD obtain frequency assignment from?
qas.a: Base level frequency manager or the AF Frequency Management Agency

qas.q: Within CONUS who is the lead for all domestic RCIED ECM operations?
What even may happen if the ECM notification card is activated?
qas.a: The FAA May issue a TFR

qas.q: What is the purpose of a SAR?
qas.a: To provide swift and accurate feedback to WOD managers to mitigate or prevent successive incidents.

qas.q: Who is normally the directing authority for the SAR?
qas.a: The AF commander excersizing ADCON and OPCON responsibility

qas.q: When filing a SAR what is the minimum requirement of the EOD team lead?
What are the three stages of a SAR?
How long after an incident does the owning EOD flight submit an ISAR?
What are the minimum requirements in an ISAR?
qas.a: A clear narrative of the incident, a description of injuries, any other information related to the incident

qas.q: What is the time frame required for a PSAR by the SAR team?
What is the final time frame for a SAR to be completed?
What are the contents of the final SAR?
qas.a:
A clear narrative of the incident,
First Hand Witness Accounts
Other Formal Reports
Injury and Illness facts
Summary list of pertinent facts
Analysis of the facts and conclusions
Incident cause and recommended preventative measures

qas.q: What should the final SAR be considered as?
Who is the office of record for a SAR?
What is the minimum security clearance level needed for EOD duty?

What is the Manpower standards for EOD flights?
Large Range:
Large Force Projection:
Small Force Projection :
In garrison:
What is the minimum sustainable manpower requirement?
At no point will EOD flights fall below this minimum requirement with MAJCOM concurrence
qas.a:
6
8 for custodial units
20 at large range bases

qas.q: How long are flights able to fall below minimum Manning requirements without requesting assistance?
What must EOD teams do prior to performing any procedures?
What are the medical response requirements for operations involving only demolition?
qas.a: Medical support within 30 minutes and trauma facilities within 60 minutes via ground or air

qas.q: What are the medical response requirements for operations involving only practice munitions?
qas.a: EMT on site or within 30 and definitive care with 60 minutes

qas.q: What are the medical response requirements involving live munitions?
qas.a: EMT on site, Trauma care within 30 and definitive care within 60

qas.q: Which AF EOD flight is required to respond to accidents/incidents
Who will be advised immediately of recovery and disposition of munitions?
Which EOD flights are required to follow RCRA?
qas.a: EOD flights in the US and it's territories

qas.q: What are the two levels of emergency response?
What is a Level 1 response indicative of?
qas.a: Immediate response with imminent and substantial danger. No permits, timelines or manifests are required

qas.q: What is a level 2 response indicative of?
qas.a: Can be delayed without comprising safety or risks. All permits and rules are usually followed

qas.q: Who can determine when the response action is complete?
What is the required response time line to the incident site during normal duty hours?
qas.a: 30 upon initial notification or 30 minutes of approval from CC as immediate response authority to DSCA

qas.q: Who will determine wether to downgrade or upgrade a response?
qas.a: The lead on site EOD team leader

qas.q: True or False: Level 1 EOD response are exempt from RCRA permits
How are emergency permits usually granted?
True or False: EOD personnel conduct planned munitions responses?
What are the four elements required to provide and EOD capability?
What are the 10 mission sets of EOD?
qas.a:
1. Aerospace Systems/ conventional munitions
2. CIED
3. WMD
4. Nuclear Weapon Response
5. UXO Recovery
6. Range Clearance
7. Mortuary Services
8. DSCA
9. IW
10. VIP Protective Support

qas.q: What will Personnel not enter into EODIMS?
What is the submit time for an incident report?
qas.a: 5 working days after response termination. 72 hours for DSCA

qas.q: What language is unsuitable for EOD explosive operations?
What is the stoplight coding for ART?
What is the breakdown coding system for ART?
qas.a:
Code Green: 100% equipment good to go
Code Yellow: some equipment is missing
Code Red: most or all equipment is missing

qas.q: What is used to train and document home station requirements?
What is the minimum requirement for broken arrow exercises?
What is the required response time line to an incident site during non duty hours?
qas.a: Responding to the scene within 60 minutes after initial notification or within 60 of approval from CC as immediate response authority to DSCA
